sections.label: Introduction
sections.text: A Rest-API framework that will boost the development, Secure the production, and ensures the scalability of your project. Chasi is modeled after an auto-mobile production, which may start with the chassis, and serve's as the car's foundation. Engineers can then layout plan/layout/design which engine to use, wheels, transmissions, electrical components, and some other modules.

requirements.label: Node
requirements.text: Minimum Node.js version required to run chasi-ts.

requirements.label: NPM
requirements.text: Minimum NPM version required to install and manage chasi-ts dependencies.

requirements.label: Git
requirements.text: Minimum Git version required for chasi-ts project management.

glossary.label: Initializing npm
glossary.text: this is the most recommended way of initializing chasi, please note that this step requires node, npm and git installed in your environment.

glossary.label: via chasi-cli
glossary.text: this requires chasi-cli to be globally installed on the system.

glossary.label: Github
glossary.text: via Github fork/clone, etc.

glossary.label: Getting Started
glossary.text: there is a template of environment file right after installation (./template.env), which can be copied as a reference for setting up a .env file.

glossary.label: Configuration
glossary.text: configurations applied to the server

glossary.label: SSR
glossary.text: Chasi's server side rendering is powered by vitejs, a powerful compiler that uses roll-up to compile files. checkout ref: https://vitejs.dev/ to learn more.

glossary.label: port
glossary.text: This value will be passed to Chasi's core upon booting, and will serve as local serving port.

glossary.label: environment
glossary.text: Server environment to be used.

glossary.label: cors
glossary.text: this object will be passed to NPM[Cors] package. reference: https://www.npmjs.com/package/cors

glossary.label: serviceCluster
glossary.text: Enables Clustering for the api Chasi instace will be invoked on the number of workers decalared at serviceCluster[workers], it will be equal to the available cpus by default.

glossary.label: hooks
glossary.text: interact with Chasi's lifecycle

glossary.label: modes
glossary.text: for setting up a specific server environment and add that inside [modes]property change the [environment] property to a specific selection, please note that the selected environment must be registered in this property.

glossary.label: Container
glossary.text: Container configuration is for managing the container registry.

glossary.label: Name
glossary.text: target name for the APP/API/etc.

glossary.label: ControllerDir
glossary.text: Default Controllers Directory.

glossary.label: ServiceBootstrap
glossary.text: this is an outside chasi module registry, ideally a logic gate in between controller's request and response, socket server's logic.

glossary.label: Middlewares
glossary.text: Middlewares must be registered here before it can be applied to any routing container, group, endpoint, or even in the websockets or channels.

glossary.label: Server Start
glossary.text: state[0] server @start lifecycle.

glossary.label: Server Initialize
glossary.text: state[1|2] server @init lifecycle, which initializes DB and routers.

glossary.label: Server boot
glossary.text: state[3] server @boot or server readystate lifecycle.

glossary.label: Router Namespace
glossary.text: A file where routing logic can be applied.

glossary.label: Router Group
glossary.text: Route group is an endpoint wrapper which applies declared options to the wrapped endpoint.

glossary.label: middleware
glossary.text: array of middleware names, middlewares listed under this group will be implemented across the group endpoints

glossary.label: prefix
glossary.text: value will be prepended to the endpoint path.

glossary.label: controller
glossary.text: controller path must be under (./config/container.ts[ControllerDir]) path declaration.

glossary.label: before
glossary.text: before() ev will be emitted before function/controller execution

glossary.label: after
glossary.text: after() will be emitted before sending a response object to the client

glossary.label: Router Endpoint
glossary.text: Routing endpoint points to a callback function, which can be a Controller Method or a callback function.

glossary.label: RouterServiceProvider
glossary.text: In RouterServiceProvider file, Routers are registered inside the boot method.

glossary.label: Router configuraion type
glossary.text: router type declaration

glossary.label: name
glossary.text: the value will be used as an index and must be unique.

glossary.label: auth
glossary.text: authentication driver name, or false/null to disable auth for this router.

glossary.label: prefix
glossary.text: value will be appended to all the routes that will be registered under a router's instance

glossary.label: namespace
glossary.text: a string path to a router container file.

glossary.label: middleware
glossary.text: middleware/s listed under this property will be implemented to all routes in the instance.

glossary.label: controllerdir
glossary.text: a string path/s that will be added to router's controller path registry.

glossary.label: AuthRouteExceptions?
glossary.text: Option to excempt a route endpoint/s from AuthGuards.

glossary.label: data?
glossary.text: router hook for shared data available throughout the Router instance.

glossary.label: mount?
glossary.text: a router hook where third party modules can be registered/mounted

glossary.label: before?
glossary.text: this function will be invoke before a request is passed into controller/handler function.

glossary.label: after?
glossary.text: this function will be invoked before a request is sent back to the client.

glossary.label: displayLog?
glossary.text: an option to print route information in the terminal.

glossary.label: Controller
glossary.text: Handles incoming request and client response, Controllers are the process in between the logic of an endpoint.

glossary.label: Database
glossary.text: Chasi Database Handler automatically connects on the declared named connection in [DatabaseConfig.connections].

glossary.label: Model
glossary.text: Chasi Model wraps and extends Mongoose Model, and will inherit mongoose model methods.

glossary.label: Model Connection
glossary.text: For multiple database connection, a connection can be specified by adding a third argument in the Model.connect() wrapper.

glossary.label: Host
glossary.text: A connection name to connect.

glossary.label: BootWithDB
glossary.text: If enabled, chasi will throw an execution error if one of the DBConnections failed.

glossary.label: hideLogConnectionStrings
glossary.text: Hides the connection string

glossary.label: default
glossary.text: The default connection to be used by the models if none is specified

glossary.label: Connections
glossary.text: A database connection declaration, entries will be process in DB Handler's registry and will try to connect upon boot.

glossary.label: express
glossary.text: Express is a minimal and flexible Node.js web application framework.

glossary.label: ServiceProvider
glossary.text: A way to manage inner/outer modules.

glossary.label: CompilerEngine
glossary.text: chasi internal module that handles SSR and frontend stuff

glossary.label: HTTP Method
glossary.text: HTTP method of an endpoint, e.g. (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS, PATCH, SEARCH).

glossary.label: Vitest
glossary.text: A native test runner that magically works with TypeScript. https://vitest.dev/

glossary.label: TDD on Chasi
glossary.text: Test-Driven Development practice is fully supported by Chasi, Powered by vitest.

glossary.label: vitest.config.ts
glossary.text: Vitest can be configured at [<root>/vitest.config.ts].

glossary.label: test environment
glossary.text: A separate Environment for testing.

glossary.label: Testing /users/*
glossary.text: Suppose we have a group of endpoints to test into our api, let's say /users endpoint.
